title: Collection Functions
description: Filter and transform data like a pro.

slides.title: Filtering Magic 🪄
slides.content:
## Sifting Data

Kotlin Collections have incredible built-in superpowers. 
Instead of writing complex `for` loops and `if` statements to find items, you can use `.filter {}` to instantly isolate exactly what you want.

quizData.question: If you write `listOf(10, 20, 30).filter { it == 20 }`, what is the resulting separated list?
quizData.explanation: The `.filter {}` block keeps only exactly the items that return True for the given condition (specifically equalling 20).

slides.title: Mapping Magic ✨
slides.content:
## Transforming Data

Use `.map {}` if you want to mutate/transform every single item in the entire list simultaneously. It applies the math to every item and returns a brand-new list.

slides.title: The 'it' Keyword 🗝️
slides.content:
## The Hidden Variable

Whenever you use functions like `.filter {}` or `.map {}`, Kotlin automatically loops behind the scenes.

Inside the `{}` brackets, the keyword `it` is a magic temporary variable that perfectly represents the current item being processed during that exact fractional loop cycle.
